```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)

total_top_4_counts = sum(sorted(counts.values(), reverse=True)[:4])

assert good_in_top_4 >= 1, f"At least one good state should be among top 4 most probable states"
assert top_4_probability <= 0.8, f"Top 4 states have probability {top_4_probability:.3f}, distribution should not be too concentrated on few states"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that the optimal states have significant probability mass
assert good_probability >= 0.3, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low"

# Check that we're not uniformly distributed (which would give ~0.03 per state)
max_single_state_prob = max(counts.values()) / shots
assert max_single_state_prob >= 0.1, f"No state has significant probability mass: max = {max_single_state_prob:.3f}"

# Verify the good states are among the most probable
sorted_states = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_states = {state for state, _ in sorted_states[:4]}
assert len(good_states.intersection(top_states)) >= 1, "At least one optimal state should be in top 4 most probable"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots
```

```python
# Check that probability is concentrated on optimal states
# For a 6-layer QAOA with optimized parameters, we expect significant concentration
assert good_probability > 0.3, f"Good state probability {good_probability:.3f} too low, expected > 0.3"

# Check that the two optimal states have reasonable balance
state_010101 = counts.get("010101", 0) / shots
state_101010 = counts.get("101010", 0) / shots
max_good_prob = max(state_010101, state_101010)
min_good_prob = min(state_010101, state_101010)

# Neither optimal state should be completely neglected if good_probability is reasonable
if good_probability > 0.1:
    assert min_good_prob > 0.01, f"One optimal state too rare: {state_010101:.3f}, {state_101010:.3f}"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that optimal states get at least 20% of the probability mass
assert good_probability >= 0.2, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} should be >= 0.2"

# Check that no single non-optimal state dominates the distribution
max_bad_state_prob = 0
for state, count in counts.items():
    if state not in good_states:
        prob = count / shots
        if prob > max_bad_state_prob:
            max_bad_state_prob = prob

assert max_bad_state_prob < 0.3, f"Max bad state probability {max_bad_state_prob:.3f} should be < 0.3"
```

```python
non_optimal_in_top = [state for state, count in top_states if state not in good_states]
max_non_optimal_prob = max((counts.get(state, 0) / shots for state in non_optimal_in_top), default=0)
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that the optimal states get significant probability mass
assert good_probability >= 0.1, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low"

# Check that no single bad state dominates the distribution
max_bad_probability = 0
for state, count in counts.items():
    if state not in good_states:
        bad_prob = count / shots
        max_bad_probability = max(max_bad_probability, bad_prob)

assert max_bad_probability <= 0.3, f"Single bad state probability {max_bad_probability:.3f} is too high"

# Check that good states combined are competitive with the most frequent state
max_state_count = max(counts.values()) if counts else 0
max_state_probability = max_state_count / shots
assert good_probability >= 0.3 * max_state_probability, f"Good states are not competitive: {good_probability:.3f} vs {max_state_probability:.3f}"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that at least 20% of measurements are on optimal alternating cuts
assert good_probability >= 0.2, f"Good state probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low"

# Check that the two optimal states together get more probability than any single non-optimal state
max_bad_count = max(counts.get(state, 0) for state in counts if state not in good_states)
max_bad_probability = max_bad_count / shots
assert good_probability > max_bad_probability, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} should exceed max bad state probability {max_bad_probability:.3f}"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# For a 6-layer QAOA on max-cut, we expect significant concentration on optimal states
# The optimal states should capture at least 20% of the probability mass
assert good_probability >= 0.2, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low, expected >= 0.2"

# Check that we're not getting uniform distribution (would be 1/32 ≈ 0.031 per state)
# Good states should have much higher probability than uniform
uniform_prob_per_state = 1.0 / 64  # 2^6 possible states
expected_good_prob_uniform = 2 * uniform_prob_per_state  # two good states
assert good_probability > 5 * expected_good_prob_uniform, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} should be much higher than uniform {expected_good_prob_uniform:.3f}"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that at least 20% of measurements are on optimal states
assert good_probability >= 0.2, f"Good state probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low"

# Check that the distribution isn't completely uniform (would be 1/64 ≈ 0.016 per state)
# If it were uniform, good states would have ~3.1% probability total
assert good_probability >= 0.05, f"Distribution appears too uniform, good probability: {good_probability:.3f}"

# Verify the good states are actually the most probable states
all_probs = [(state, count/shots) for state, count in counts.items()]
all_probs.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_states = {state for state, prob in all_probs[:4]}
assert len(good_states.intersection(top_states)) >= 1, "At least one optimal state should be in top 4 most probable"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# For a 6-layer QAOA with optimized parameters, we expect significant concentration
# on the optimal alternating cut states (010101 and 101010)
assert good_probability > 0.15, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low, indicating heavy leakage to non-optimal states"

# Check that we're not stuck in a completely uniform distribution
# (which would give each of the 2^6 = 64 states probability 1/64 ≈ 0.0156)
max_state_count = max(counts.values()) if counts else 0
max_state_probability = max_state_count / shots
assert max_state_probability > 0.05, f"Maximum state probability {max_state_probability:.3f} is too low, suggesting near-uniform distribution"

# Verify that at least one of the optimal states has reasonable probability
individual_good_probs = [counts.get(state, 0) / shots for state in good_states]
max_good_prob = max(individual_good_probs) if individual_good_probs else 0
assert max_good_prob > 0.05, f"Best individual good state probability {max_good_prob:.3f} is too low"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that the optimal states have significant probability mass
assert good_probability > 0.1, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} is too low"

# Check that the distribution is reasonably concentrated on good states
# (not completely uniform across all 64 possible states)
total_states_with_counts = len([state for state in counts if counts[state] > 0])
assert total_states_with_counts < 32, f"Distribution too spread out: {total_states_with_counts} states measured"

# Verify the good states are among the most probable outcomes
sorted_states = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_states = [state for state, _ in sorted_states[:4]]
assert any(state in good_states for state in top_states), "No optimal states in top 4 most probable outcomes"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots
assert good_probability > 0.15, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} should be > 0.15"

total_counted = sum(counts.values())
assert abs(total_counted - shots) / shots < 0.01, f"Total counts {total_counted} should approximately equal shots {shots}"

max_single_bad_state_prob = 0
for state, count in counts.items():
    if state not in good_states:
        prob = count / shots
        if prob > max_single_bad_state_prob:
            max_single_bad_state_prob = prob

assert max_single_bad_state_prob < 0.1, f"No single bad state should have probability > 0.1, found {max_single_bad_state_prob:.3f}"
```

```python
good_states = {"010101", "101010"}
good_counts = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in good_states)
good_probability = good_counts / shots

# Check that the optimal states have significant probability mass
assert good_probability > 0.15, f"Good states probability {good_probability:.3f} too low"

# Check that we're not uniform (which would give ~0.03 for good states)
assert good_probability > 0.05, f"Distribution appears too uniform, good probability {good_probability:.3f}"

# Verify the two optimal states are the most probable overall
all_probs = [(state, count/shots) for state, count in counts.items()]
all_probs.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_two_states = {all_probs[0][0], all_probs[1][0]}
assert good_states.issubset(top_two_states) or len(good_states.intersection(top_two_states)) >= 1, "At least one optimal state should be in top 2"
```
